g.text: Composite Index (a, b, c) - Leftmost Prefix Rule
Composite Index: INDEX idx_abc ON table(a, b, c)
Sorted by a (primary), then b (secondary), then c (tertiary)
WHERE a = 1 | ✓ Full index scan | a is leftmost
WHERE a = 1 AND b > 5 | ✓ Index range scan | (a, b) prefix
WHERE a = 1 AND b = 2 AND c > 10 | ✓ Full index scan | (a, b, c) prefix
WHERE b = 5 | ✗ Full table scan | b not leftmost - no prefix
WHERE b = 5 AND c > 10 | ✗ Full table scan | missing a prefix
WHERE c > 10 | ✗ Full table scan | c not leftmost

div.p:
Index on (a, b, c) supports: ✓ WHERE a = X ✓ WHERE a = X AND b = Y
✓ WHERE a = X AND b > Y ✓ WHERE a = X AND b = Y AND c = Z Cannot use index: ✗ WHERE b = Y ✗ WHERE b = Y AND c = Z ✗ WHERE c = Z Must include ALL leftmost columns
